title: Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) Headers
description:
Implement a Content Security Policy (CSP) to define allowed sources for each type of content (scripts, images, styles, etc.), defending against XSS attacks and controlling browser security behavior.

## CSP Directives Required

- **default-src**: Restrict to `self` by default
- **script-src**: Whitelist approved script sources (jQuery CDN, Heap Analytics, Pendo, etc.)
- **img-src**: Allow images from `self`, data URIs, and trusted domains
- **style-src**: Allow styles from `self`, Google Fonts, CDN sources
- **form-action**: Restrict to `self` and Salesforce domains (with wildcard for customer subdomains)
- **frame-src**: Allow YouTube embeds
- **object-src**: Set to `none`

## Challenges

- Inline styles had to be converted to CSS classes to comply with CSP directives.
- Form submissions were initially blocked by CSP, requiring Salesforce domain whitelisting.
- Each Salesforce customer has a unique subdomain, requiring a wildcard `*.salesforce.com` in the CSP.
- Login flow between Production and Sandbox Salesforce environments required specific domain entries.
acceptance_criteria: CSP header is set on all HTTP responses
External scripts load only from whitelisted CDN sources
Inline styles are replaced with CSS classes where CSP requires it
Salesforce OAuth login works for both Production and Sandbox environments
Wildcard *.salesforce.com is included to support all customer subdomains
No CSP violation errors appear in browser console during normal usage
middleware_changes: CSP header added via Laravel middleware applied to all HTTP responses. Inline styles across multiple pages converted to CSS class references. Nonce-based script allowlisting implemented for inline scripts.
implementation_notes: Implementation required removing inline styles across dashboard pages and converting them to CSS classes. The Salesforce login form was initially blocked by CSP because the form-action directive didn't include the Salesforce domain. A wildcard *.salesforce.com was added to support multi-tenant customer subdomains. CSP was implemented via Laravel middleware for centralized control.
comment_summary: Developer implemented CSP but encountered issues with inline styles and Salesforce OAuth form submissions being blocked. Inline styles were converted to CSS classes. A login issue where Production login was blocked but Sandbox worked was resolved by adding the specific Salesforce dev-ed domain. Later, a wildcard *.salesforce.com was added to handle all customer subdomains. Product owner confirmed everything worked after testing in incognito mode.
